page: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The instance provides 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes a large L3 cache of 480 MB, alongside 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 4 Gbps, and no complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8), which accelerate cryptographic, vector, and matrix math operations.

Benchmark results show strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores placing in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also demonstrate strong performance in the top 25% tier. For machine learning workloads, the hardware achieves top-tier performance in text generation for small LLM models (135M) at 388.45 tokens per second, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for general-purpose workloads, web serving, text processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.4xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed. Equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, it provides a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core and text processing capabilities, alongside strong single-threaded compression speeds. The processor's support for Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) enables efficient execution of vector and matrix operations. This instance is well-suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, text processing, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier single-core and text processing performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.9 GHz processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory, strong single-core performance, and advanced Intel matrix math acceleration.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 64 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector math
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Best suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and light inference
